sections.paragraph:
Βηθζαθά, ἡ indecl. Bethzatha J 5:2 (Eus., Onom. 58, 2; also D Βελζεθα); s. Βηθεσδά. Acc. to 1 Macc 7:19 and Jos., Bell. 2, 328; 530; 5, 149; 151; 246 Bethz. is the name of the northern extension of the city, which may give a hint as to the location of the pool.—HVincent and FAbel, Jérusalem I 1912, 685ff; GDalman, Jerus. u. s. Gelände 1930. On the name s. FBurkitt, The Syriac Forms of NT Proper Names 1912, 20f; BHHW I 232f.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

NT NT = New Testament

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 174
